perturbation.original_text: Except in connection with any joint project between Cyberlux and ICT, the receiving party shall not make any use of the disclosing party's Proprietary Information for is own benefit or for the benefit of any other individual, corporation or entity.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall make use of the disclosing party's Proprietary Information for its own benefit or for the benefit of any other individual, corporation or entity.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that, except for joint projects, the receiving party 'shall not make any use' of the disclosing party's proprietary information. The modified clause removes the exception for joint projects and states that the receiving party 'shall make use' of the proprietary information. This directly contradicts the original intention of limited usage and creates uncertainty about the permissible uses of Proprietary Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party shall make use of the disclosing party's Proprietary Information for its own benefit or for the benefit of any other individual, corporation or entity.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the use of proprietary information, stating the receiving party 'shall not make any use,' except for joint projects. The changed text removes this restriction and explicitly states the receiving party 'shall make use' of the proprietary information. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, confirming the direct contradiction. The change directly alters the obligation from prohibition to requirement, creating a strong and clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each receiving party acknowledges that the Proprietary Information of the disclosing party is central to the disclosing party's business and was developed by or for the disclosing party at a significant cost. Each receiving party further acknowledges that damages would not be an adequate remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives and that the disclosing party may obtain injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or any of its Representatives. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for .any such breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each receiving party acknowledges that the Proprietary Information of the disclosing party may not be central to the disclosing party's business and was not necessarily developed by or for the disclosing party at a significant cost. Each receiving party further acknowledges that damages may be an adequate remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives and that the disclosing party may not obtain injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or any of its Representatives. Such remedy shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any such breach of this Agreement, and shall not be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause underscores the importance and value of Proprietary Information, establishing that monetary damages are insufficient and injunctive relief is appropriate. The modified text undermines this by suggesting the information might not be central or costly and that damages might suffice. It also states that injunctive relief may not be obtained and that the remedy is exclusive. This creates an internal conflict regarding the remedies available and the seriousness of breaching the agreement. This implies a lesser standard on the protection of Proprietary Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each receiving party acknowledges that the Proprietary Information of the disclosing party may not be central to the disclosing party's business and was not necessarily developed by or for the disclosing party at a significant cost. Each receiving party further acknowledges that damages may be an adequate remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives and that the disclosing party may not obtain injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or any of its Representatives. Such remedy shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any such breach of this Agreement, and shall not be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, as it is literally the same text, with contradictory meanings within the same paragraph. The original text established the proprietary information as central to the disclosing party's business and worthy of injunctive relief, whereas the changed text explicitly states it may not be central and injunctive relief may not be obtained. The remedies are then deemed to be exclusive which contrasts with the initial statement. This creates a clear and strong in-text contradiction regarding the importance and protection of the proprietary information and the remedies available, adhering to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
